  "findings": [
    "Flashing is not lapped or sealed at joints, allowing water penetration behind the masonry veneer."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Lap flashing joints a minimum of 6 inches and seal them with a compatible adhesive/sealant before closing in the wall."
  ],
  "rationale": "According to TMS 402/602 and BIA Technical Notes 7, flashing must be continuous, lapped at least 6 inches, and sealed at all joints to prevent water infiltration into the building envelope. Unsealed flashing fails to perform its primary function of directing moisture to the exterior via weep holes.",
  "references": [
    "TMS 402/602",
    "BIA Technical Note 7"
  ]
}
